task_completion.description: Agent explores the OpenICE source code to find DDS data model specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal, file explorer, or text editor showing the directory /opt/openice/mdpnp/ or specifically viewing files like 'ice.idl' or Java files in the 'interop-lab' folder.

task_completion.description: Agent observes simulated devices in the running OpenICE Supervisor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor graphical user interface (GUI) is open and running, displaying simulated medical devices and their data.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the data dictionary file with at least 3 distinct DDS data topic types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing a file named 'openice_data_dictionary.txt' on the Desktop containing documentation for at least 3 valid OpenICE types (e.g., Numeric, SampleArray, DeviceIdentity, AlarmLimit).

task_completion.description: The data dictionary includes required details: full type names, field names with data types, clinical purposes, and example metric IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within 'openice_data_dictionary.txt', there are clear sections or tables listing specific fields (e.g., unique_device_identifier, metric_id, value), their data types, a description of their clinical purpose, and example metric IDs.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the FHIR mapping proposal file mapping OpenICE types to HL7 FHIR R4 resources.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing a file named 'fhir_mapping_proposal.txt' on the Desktop that references FHIR R4 resources (e.g., Observation, Device, Patient, MedicationAdministration).

task_completion.description: The FHIR mapping proposal includes field-level mapping tables and transformation requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within 'fhir_mapping_proposal.txt', there are tables or structured lists showing how specific OpenICE fields map to specific FHIR resource elements, along with notes on data transformation requirements.

integrity.description: Agent actually inspected the source code files rather than relying solely on pre-trained knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent opening and reading the actual source files (like ice.idl) using commands like 'cat', 'less', or a text editor, rather than just immediately writing the output files.

integrity.description: Agent did not include hallucinated data types or fields that do not exist in the OpenICE source code.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files MUST NOT contain the types 'GlobalSimulationObjective' or 'LocalModeObjective', and MUST NOT contain the field 'millisecondsPerSample'.

integrity.description: The generated files appear substantial enough to meet the minimum byte requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'openice_data_dictionary.txt' and 'fhir_mapping_proposal.txt' files should contain multiple paragraphs, lists, or tables, clearly exceeding a few simple sentences (plausibly >500 and >400 bytes respectively).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Valid OpenICE DDS types found in the source code (ice.idl) include: Numeric, SampleArray, AlarmLimit, InfusionObjective, DeviceIdentity, Patient, InfusionStatus, and DeviceConnectivity. Valid fields include: unique_device_identifier, metric_id, vendor_metric_id, instance_id, unit_id, value, values, device_time, presentation_time, frequency, manufacturer, model, and serial_number. Valid FHIR R4 resources include: Observation, Device, Patient, MedicationAdministration, DeviceMetric, RiskAssessment, MedicationRequest, and DeviceUseStatement. CRITICAL: The agent MUST NOT document 'GlobalSimulationObjective', 'LocalModeObjective', or the field 'millisecondsPerSample', as these are verified to NOT exist in the OpenICE source code.
